name: Network Sniffing - Linux
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Network Sniffing - Linux
SIGMA Description: Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection.
An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.


False positives:
- Legitimate administrator or user uses network sniffing tool for legitimate reasons.

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%tcpdump%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-c%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a3%-i%') OR (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%tshark%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-c%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a3%-i%')) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Network Sniffing - Linux on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%tcpdump%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-c%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a3%-i%') OR (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%tshark%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-c%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a3%-i%')) AQL filter query
